content.text:
Course:
Business Ethics (BE) — Semester 6, Unit UT6, Credits: 2, Coeff: 1
Course Objectives
The syllabus describes the concept of business ethics required in the workplace, highlighting its sources and how to instill it within human resources. It also addresses workplace discipline and social responsibility of business organizations.
The student will acquire work ethics and understand its positive role in reducing the phenomenon of corruption.
The student will be able to recognize the intricacies of corruption, its harms, and ways to combat it.
Understanding the values of the individual and the organization and their impact on the performance of individuals and organizations.
Providing the student with the concept of the necessity of discipline and professional commitment, and the factors of work ethics in terms of responsibility and public interest.
Managing patterns and elements of social responsibility.
Defining the code of conduct, general principles, and legal articles that govern the rules of employee conduct.
Identifying the consequences of unethical business activities on community development.
Course Content
Introduction to Business Ethics
Sources of Business Ethics and Methods of Developing Them
Professional Ethics and the Code of Ethics
Business Ethics and Corporate Functions
Business Ethics and Managerial Functions
Introduction to Administrative Corruption
Dimensions of Administrative Corruption and Methods of Combating It
Corruption Indicators and Governance
Business Ethics, Governance, and Social Responsibility
Organizational Culture and Business Ethics
Teaching Mode
Hybrid (20% face to face and 80% online)
Assessment Mode
Final exam (100%)
Recommended Textbooks
Blackburn, S. (2001). Ethics: A very short introduction. Oxford University Press.
Aristotle. (2009). The Nicomachean ethics (W. D. Ross, Trans.). Oxford University Press.
Kant, I. (1997). Groundwork of the metaphysics of morals. Cambridge University Press.
MacKinnon, B., & Fiala, A. (2018). Ethics: Theory and contemporary issues (9th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Deigh, J. (2010). An introduction to ethics. Cambridge University Press.
Rachels, J., & Rachels, S. (2018). The elements of moral philosophy (9th ed.). McGraw-Hill Education.
Singer, P. (Ed.). (2013). A companion to ethics. Wiley-Blackwell.
